steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens on Nixon's Watergate scandal, then pivots with "But Nixon's legacy is complex" — signaling a balanced view that weighs both failings and achievements (EPA, healthcare reform). The question asks for the main idea the passage conveys.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'both achievements and failings': captures the balanced structure the passage explicitly builds. ✓
  • Choice B — 'time passage': introduces a claim about how views change over time; the passage makes no such argument. ✗
  • Choice C — 'prominence due to associates': wrongly attributes his historical significance to the people around him rather than to his own record. ✗
  • Choice D — 'achievements only': ignores the Watergate scandal entirely, contradicting the passage's explicit acknowledgment of his failings. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
